title: CLAUDE.md
text: sora — Shared Objects R Applications. Uses POSIX shared memory (Linux, macOS) Win32 file mappings (Windows) R’s ALTREP framework let multiple processes machine read physical memory pages. external dependencies. Requires R >= 4.3.0 (ALTLIST). API: sora() → ALTREP shared object, map_shared() → open SHM name, shared_name() → extract SHM name, is_shared() → test shared. SHM lifetime automatic — managed R’s garbage collector via chained external pointer finalizers. ALTREP serialization hooks serialize standalone shared objects SHM name (~30 bytes) ALTLIST element vectors (parent_name, index), enabling transparent use mirai R serialization path.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Set NOT_CRAN=true environment variable run integration tests require mirai daemons.
code: # Run unit tests (single file, minitest framework — not testthat) source("tests/tests.R")

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Build package R CMD build .  # Check package R CMD check --no-manual sora_*.tar.gz # Generate documentation from roxygen2 comments devtools::document()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tier 2 (zero-copy): atomic vectors (including character vectors arbitrary attributes names, class, levels, dim) data frame columns written directly SHM backed ALTREP consumers. Attributes serialized trailing section SHM region restored via SET_ATTRIB consumer. numeric types, Dataptr_or_null returns SHM pointer reads; Dataptr(writable=TRUE) materializes private copy (COW). character vectors, Elt lazily creates CHARSXP via Rf_mkCharLenCE SHM data; Dataptr_or_null returns NULL force element--element access. Tier 1 (pass-): R objects (environments, closures, language objects) returned unchanged sora(). SHM created.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R exported functions single .Call wrappers. sora() calls sora_create_call dispatches TYPEOF(x): NILSXP → returned -(falls checks). VECSXP/LISTSXP → sora_shm_create_list_call — ALTLIST per-element directory. element independently Tier 2 (atomic, without attributes) Tier 1 (serialized). Data frames pairlists go path (pairlists coerced VECSXP via Rf_coerceVector C level). STRSXP → sora_shm_create_string_call — ALTSTRING backed SHM offset table + packed string data. Attributes () serialized string data. Tier 2 eligible types (REALSXP, INTSXP, LGLSXP, RAWSXP, CPLXSXP) → sora_shm_create_vector_call — single ALTREP vector backed SHM. Attributes () serialized vector data. Everything else → returned -(Tier 1 pass-). creation path returns ALTREP result via sora_make_result, chains host extptr (responsible shm_unlink) ALTREP’s external pointer hierarchy. SHM lifetime thus tied R object — ALTREP garbage collected, munmap unlink happen automatically.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: ALTREP classes register Serialized_state Unserialize methods. Standalone shared objects (created sora() map_shared()) serialize just SHM name string (~30 bytes). unserialize, sora_Unserialize validates name via sora_is_shm_name() (checks /sora_ prefix POSIX, Local\sora_ Windows), sora_shm_open_and_wrap_call opens SHM creates fresh ALTREP wrapper. R’s ALTREP serialization framework separately serializes restores object’s attributes. Element vectors ALTLIST serialize list(parent_name, index). unserialize, sora_Unserialize validates element types (first element STRSXP, second INTSXP) treating element reference, sora_open_element opens parent SHM extracts element index (including restoring per-element attributes directory’s attrs_size field). element’s sora_vec/sora_str struct stores index field (int32_t, -1 standalone, >= 0 ALTLIST elements). Fallback full materialization : - COW-materialized vectors (data2 set) - SHM mapping already closed (extptr cleared)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: first 4 bytes SHM region identify layout: - 0x534F524C (“SORL”): ALTLIST — contains element directory + per-element data - 0x534F5248 (“SORH”): ALTREP vector — 64-byte header + data + optional serialized attributes - 0x534F5253 (“SORS”): ALTSTRING — 24-byte header + offset table + packed strings + optional serialized attributes

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Atomic vector (SORH): 64-byte header, data starts byte 64 (64-byte aligned SIMD). Serialized attributes () follow vector data. attributes (names, dim, class, levels, tzone, etc.) serialized pairlist trailing attrs section. consumer, sora_restore_attrs unserializes sets via SET_ATTRIB. ALTLIST (SORL): Header + element directory + per-element data regions. element directory entry (32 bytes): data_offset(8) + data_size(8) + sexptype(4) + attrs_size(4) + length(8). sexptype != 0 means Tier 2 (raw data string); sexptype == 0 means Tier 1 (serialized bytes). sexptype == STRSXP indicates string element whose data region contains offset table + packed strings. attrs_size > 0, element’s serialized attributes appended raw data within data region (offset data_offset + data_size - attrs_size). consumer, sora_restore_attrs unserializes applies . String vector (SORS): 24-byte header + offset table + packed string data + optional serialized attributes. offset table entry (16 bytes): str_offset(int64) + str_length(int32) + str_encoding(int32). str_offset relative start packed string area. str_length < 0 means NA_STRING. str_encoding cetype_t value (0=native, 1=UTF-8, 2=Latin-1, 3=bytes). layout used string elements within ALTLIST regions (offset table starts element’s data_offset; element-level attrs use directory entry’s attrs_size field).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: sora_tag (C global, altrep.c): Interned symbol Rf_install("sora"). Serves two roles: (1) tag every SHM extptr is_shared() identification, (2) ALTLIST cache sentinel distinguish “yet accessed” cached NULL element.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: SHM lifetime fully automatic, managed chaining host extptr (responsible shm_unlink/CloseHandle) ALTREP object’s external pointer hierarchy. Host side (sora): sora_shm_create allocates SHM region mmaps ; POSIX, fd closed immediately mmap (mapping stays valid; sora_shm struct retain fd). sora_make_result splits ownership: ALTREP wrapper gets mapping (addr, size) via daemon-side SHM extptr (sora_shm_finalizer → munmap), host extptr gets handle (name POSIX unlink, HANDLE Windows) via sora_host_finalizer. host extptr chained protected value ALTREP’s SHM extptr. ALTREP garbage collected, finalizers run: munmap + unlink. R_RegisterCFinalizerEx(..., TRUE) ensures cleanup session exit. Consumer side (map_shared/unserialize): sora_shm_open maps region read-(size discovered via fstat/VirtualQuery); POSIX, fd closed immediately mmap. ALTREP wrapper holds external pointer (sora_shm_finalizer) calls munmap (never unlink). ALTLIST element vectors hold sora_vec sora_str extptr whose protected value references parent SHM extptr, keeping mapping alive. Element vectors store index (int32_t) sora_vec/sora_str struct compact serialization. Element lifetime: Element vectors extracted ALTLIST keep parent SHM alive extptr chain (element → parent SHM extptr → host extptr). Even parent ALTLIST garbage collected, SHM survives long element referenced.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: sora.h: Types (sora_shm, sora_buf, sora_vec index field), function declarations, SORA_ALIGN64 macro shm.c: Platform-abstracted SHM create/open/close. Linux, uses open("/dev/shm/...") directly avoid -lrt link dependency. macOS, uses shm_open/shm_unlink (libc). Finalizers host daemon side. serialize.c: Counting pass (sora_serialize_count), fixed-buffer write (sora_serialize_into), unserialize--buffer (sora_unserialize_from), sora_sizeof_elt altrep.c: ALTREP class definitions methods, sora_make_vector/sora_make_string helpers, sora_unwrap_element helper (shared element extraction sora_list_Elt sora_open_element), sora_restore_attrs helper, sora_is_shm_name validator, unified creation dispatcher (sora_create_call), static per-type creation functions, consumer-side open+wrap dispatch (sora_shm_open_and_wrap_call), element open (sora_open_element), identity check (sora_is_shared_call), name extraction (sora_shm_name_call), serialization hooks (Serialized_state/Unserialize), sora_altrep_init init.c: R_init_sora, .Call registration table (4 entry points: sora_create, sora_shm_open_and_wrap, sora_is_shared, sora_shm_name) .Call names map C functions _call suffix (e.g., R calls sora_create → C function sora_create_call). entry points take single SEXP argument.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: sora-package.R: Package docs sora.R: four exported functions single .Call wrappers — dispatch error handling C level. sora() → sora_create, map_shared() → sora_shm_open_and_wrap, shared_name() → sora_shm_name, is_shared() → sora_is_shared.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Uses minitest, minimal framework defined top tests/tests.R: - test_true(), test_false(), test_null(), test_equal(), test_identical(), test_class(), test_error() tests run sequentially single file. Integration tests (mirai daemons) gated behind NOT_CRAN=true.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Linux: SHM lives /dev/shm/ (tmpfs). Accessed via open()/unlink() directly, avoiding -lrt link dependency shm_open requires. MAP_POPULATE pre-faults pages. macOS: Kernel-backed SHM via shm_open/shm_unlink (libc, extra link flags). MAP_POPULATE -op (defined 0). Windows: Page-file-backed via CreateFileMappingA/MapViewOfFile. kernel32 always available. Host must keep mapping handle alive consumers opened (GC-chained host extptr handles automatically).

code: @Manual{,   title = {sora: Shared Objects for R Applications},   author = {Charlie Gao},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1.9000},   url = {https://shikokuchuo.github.io/sora/}, }

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: Shared Objects R Applications → sora() writes R object shared memory returns shared version → ALTREP serialization hooks — shared objects serialize compactly → ALTREP-backed lazy access — columns materialize first touch, → OS-level shared memory (POSIX / Win32), external dependencies → Automatic cleanup — shared memory freed R’s garbage collector

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: sora() writes R object shared memory returns shared version backed zero-copy ALTREP. Shared objects serialize compactly via ALTREP serialization hooks, working transparently mirai R serialization path. Shared memory automatically freed object garbage collected.
code: library(sora)  # Share a vector — returns an ALTREP-backed object x <- sora(rnorm(1e6)) mean(x) #> [1] -0.0009981362  # Serialized form is ~100 bytes, not ~8 MB length(serialize(x, NULL)) #> [1] 124

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: shared_name() extracts SHM name shared object. map_shared() opens shared region name — useful accessing data another process without serialization:
code: x <- sora(1:1e6)  # Extract the SHM name nm <- shared_name(x) nm #> [1] "/sora_4c6e_1"  # Another process can map the same region by name y <- map_shared(nm) identical(x[], y[]) #> [1] TRUE

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: Shared objects can sent local daemons — ALTREP serialization hooks ensure SHM name crosses wire, worker maps physical memory. Elements shared list also serialize compactly — element travels reference position parent shared region, full data:
code: library(lobstr) library(mirai)  daemons(1)  x <- sora(rnorm(1e6))  # Worker maps the same shared memory — 0 bytes copied m <- mirai(list(mean = mean(x), size = lobstr::obj_size(x)), x = x) m[] #> $mean #> [1] 0.001275904 #>  #> $size #> 792 B  daemons(0) daemons(4)  # Share a list — all 4 vectors in a single shared region x <- sora(list(a = rnorm(1e6), b = rnorm(1e6), c = rnorm(1e6), d = rnorm(1e6)))  # Each element is sent as (parent_name, index) — zero-copy on the worker mirai_map(x, \(v) list(mean = mean(v), size = lobstr::obj_size(v)))[.flat] #> $a.mean #> [1] 5.630673e-06 #>  #> $a.size #> 728 B #>  #> $b.mean #> [1] -0.0009973658 #>  #> $b.size #> 728 B #>  #> $c.mean #> [1] -0.0001679982 #>  #> $c.size #> 728 B #>  #> $d.mean #> [1] -0.0001814566 #>  #> $d.size #> 728 B  daemons(0)

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: Parallel computing multiplies memory. 8 workers need 210 MB dataset, 1.7 GB serialization, transfer, deserialization — plus 8 separate copies consuming RAM. sora eliminates . sora() writes data shared memory . worker maps physical pages, receiving reference ~300 bytes instead full dataset — 700,000x reduction: Memory savings large translate directly performance. Less serialization, less transfer, less allocation — worker starts computing sooner system spends less time moving data:
code: n <- 5000000L  set.seed(42) df <- data.frame(   x1 = rnorm(n), x2 = rnorm(n), x3 = rnorm(n),   x4 = runif(n), x5 = runif(n),   group = sample.int(100L, n, replace = TRUE) )  shared_df <- sora(df)  # Per-task payload: ~210 MB vs ~300 bytes length(serialize(df, NULL)) #> [1] 220000263 length(serialize(shared_df, NULL)) #> [1] 304 boot_means <- function(seed, data) {   set.seed(seed)   idx <- sample.int(nrow(data), replace = TRUE)   colMeans(data[idx, ]) }  seeds <- seq_len(8L) daemons(8)  # Without sora — each daemon deserializes a full copy system.time(mirai_map(seeds, boot_means, data = df)[]) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   2.381  45.335   6.613  # With sora — each daemon maps the same shared memory system.time(mirai_map(seeds, boot_means, data = shared_df)[]) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   1.544  31.148   4.469  daemons(0)

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: atomic vectors — including character vectors attributes (factors, Dates, named vectors, etc.) — data frame columns written directly shared memory. Pairlists coerced lists handled way. sora() returns ALTREP wrappers point shared pages — deserialization, per-process memory allocation. data frame 10 columns lives single shared region; task touches 3 columns pays 3. Character strings accessed lazily per element. R objects (environments, closures, language objects) returned unchanged sora() — shared memory region created. Diagram showing sora() writing object OS-backed shared memory, memory-mapped processes using zero-copy ALTREP wrappers

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: Shared memory managed R’s garbage collector. SHM region stays alive long shared object (element extracted ) referenced R. references remain, garbage collector frees shared memory automatically. Important: Always assign result sora() variable. shared memory kept alive R object reference — result used temporary (assigned), garbage collector may free shared memory consumer process mapped .

title: Shared Objects for R Applications
text: Shared data mapped read-. Mutations always local — R’s copy--write mechanism ensures processes continue reading original shared data: Structural changes list data frame (adding, removing, reordering elements) produce regular R list. shared region unaffected. Modifying values within shared vector (e.g., X[1] <- 0) materializes just vector private copy. vectors shared region stay zero-copy. – Please note sora project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

what: sora: Shared Objects for R Applications — sora-package

Character vectors use packed layout elements materialised lazily access. serialised (e.g. serialize across mirai call), shared object represented compactly SHM name (~30 bytes) rather contents. shared memory region managed automatically. stays alive long returned object (element extracted ) referenced R, freed garbage collector references remain.
